WIN-025 includes the 2026-03-15 'dome author removed' annotation in detail_extra (previous report's moderate finding is resolved). The 'new_in_v51 asterisk' convention is not implemented in the current generate-html.js template — this is intentional (verdict-tally tiles carry the new-in-V51 information instead); not a regression.

We additionally cover WIN-057b and WIN-058b — intentional review-internal sub-splits, not missing dome content. WIN-025 is flagged 'REMOVED' on the dome site and our review already carries the matching annotation. No number collisions, no title drift.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt (5460 bytes) present, references review URL and dome model. docs/sitemap.xml (404 bytes) valid urlset with main URL. docs/robots.txt present with Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. data/uncounted-failures.json schema valid, 8 entries, no duplicates. HTML head contains meta description, og:title, og:description, and ClaimReview JSON-LD.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: CRITICAL: 5 expansion files on disk (EXP-238 through EXP-242) are NOT in monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json. max_id in tracker = 237, next_id = 238. Phase 0 invariant (next_id > max_tracker_id) passes trivially, but the file-vs-tracker invariant fails: next_id (238) <= highest EXP ID on disk (242). The next EXP allocation will produce EXP-238, which collides with an existing integrated expansion file. The decider integrated EXP-238–242 in commits 805cd55 and 098b1a8 but never wrote tracker entries or advanced next_id.

issues_found.description: 5 expansion files (EXP-238 through EXP-242) exist on disk in monitor/analyst/expansions/ but have NO entries in monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json. tracker.next_id = 238 while highest file ID = 242 — the next decider allocation will produce EXP-238 and collide with the existing integrated file. The decider's integration commits (805cd55 EXP-238; 098b1a8 EXP-239/240/241/242) updated wins.json and predictions.json but skipped the tracker write.
issues_found.suggested_fix:
Back-fill 5 tracker items and advance next_id to 243. Run in the clean clone:

node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const t=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json','utf8')); const now=new Date().toISOString(); const entries=[{id:'EXP-238',category:'win_patch',status:'integrated',output_file:'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-238-WIN025-kp-audit-iss1482.json',resolves_issues:['ISS-1482'],integrated_at:now,integrated_in:'805cd55'},{id:'EXP-239',category:'no_op',status:'integrated',output_file:'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-239-WIN025-architectural-disjoint-already-resolved-iss1486.json',resolves_issues:['ISS-1486'],no_output_file_reason:'no_op_confirmation',integrated_at:now,integrated_in:'098b1a8'},{id:'EXP-240',category:'win_patch',status:'integrated',output_file:'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-240-WIN069-circuity-citation-retraction-iss1487-iss1488.json',resolves_issues:['ISS-1487','ISS-1488'],integrated_at:now,integrated_in:'098b1a8'},{id:'EXP-241',category:'win_patch',status:'integrated',output_file:'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-241-WIN068-fsf-scope-compression-iss1490.json',resolves_issues:['ISS-1490'],integrated_at:now,integrated_in:'098b1a8'},{id:'EXP-242',category:'prediction_patch',status:'integrated',output_file:'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-242-WIN061-pred-sr-suppress-p093-reclassification-iss1489.json',resolves_issues:['ISS-1489'],integrated_at:now,integrated_in:'098b1a8'}]; t.items.push(...entries); t.next_id=243; fs.writeFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json', JSON.stringify(t,null,2));"

Then commit + push. Also investigate the decider self-apply path in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md to find why tracker-write was skipped for these 5 integrations.

issues_found.description: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c10.json (14.6 KB, 2026-04-24T13:30Z) is a real, substantive curmudgeon review analyzing post-EXP-221/223 changes to WIN-067. It exists only on the FUSE workspace and is absent from git. The processed-reviews ledger shows WIN-067.c1 through c9 processed; c10 is not. If the workspace is recycled this review is lost.
issues_found.suggested_fix: cp /sessions/festive-beautiful-volta/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c10.json /tmp/dome-integrity-clone/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c10.json && cd /tmp/dome-integrity-clone && git add monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c10.json && git commit -m 'Commit WIN-067.c10 curmudgeon review (workspace-only → git)' && git push. Then investigate workspace-sync: reviews/ is append-only and workspace-sync should have moved this file — check the direction-guard decision trace for WIN-067.c10.json in the next workspace-sync log.

issues_found.description: Two non-review junk files live under append-only directories on the workspace: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0 bytes, 2026-04-21) and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak. Neither is processed by any pipeline, but they sit in directories the digest and tracker scan. The 0-byte .json.build.js appears to be an editor temp-file leak.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Delete both. On the workspace: `allow_cowork_file_delete` (or leave them — FUSE cannot unlink, but they have unrecognized extensions so pipelines ignore them). The .bak file is recoverable from git history if ever needed.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 missing from tracker sequence (long-standing pre-Phase-0 gap). next_id invariant was intact for that slot so there's no collision risk; cosmetic only and already flagged in prior reports.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Optionally back-fill a void tombstone entry; otherwise no action.

issues_found.description: podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov GRACE ProdSpecDoc PDF continues to time out at TLS handshake — flagged on 2026-04-21, 2026-04-22, 2026-04-23, and again today. Four consecutive days now. Not our URL to fix.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Leave flagged; re-check next run. If the PDF is still unreachable after another week, consider swapping to an alternate PO.DAAC mirror or the podaac-opendap URL.

summary: Overall: fail. Build is reproducible (0 diff vs published). Test suite passes 3448/0. Data-prose, dome-site alignment, tab structure, internal links, theme colors, discoverability, digest coverage, parse errors, skip log, and CLAUDE.md all clean. Two major findings: (1) tracker/file-ID mismatch — EXP-238 through EXP-242 are integrated on disk but missing from expansion-tracker.json with next_id=238, so the next decider EXP allocation WILL collide with an existing file; this blocks the next decider run until the tracker is back-filled and next_id advanced to 243; (2) WIN-067.c10.json curmudgeon review is workspace-only and at risk of silent loss. Moderate: two junk files (one 0-byte editor leak, one .bak) sit in append-only pipeline directories and should be cleaned. Minor: PO.DAAC GRACE PDF TLS timeout now four consecutive days.
